{{Multiple issues | One source = May 2021 | Primary sources = May 2021 }} '''Kakise Kagura''' (蛎瀬神楽) is a {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽}} tradition passed down at {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja|八坂神社 (中津市蛎瀬)}} in {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|ja|中津市}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kakise Kagura is one of the {{ill|Buzen Kagura|ja|豊前神楽}}, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 2016. It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from {{ill|Mount Kubote|ja|求菩提山}}. According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]][[Category: of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神}}. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}, divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial {{ill|Five Elements|ja|五行}}. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト}} slaying the {{ill|Yamata no Orochi|ja|ヤマタノオロチ}}. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ}} are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.

also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神|12=draft|12=draft}}. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ|12=draft|12=draft}}, divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial {{ill|Five Elements|ja|五行|12=draft|12=draft}}. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=draft|12=draft}} slaying the {{ill|Yamata no Orochi|ja|ヤマタノオロチ|12=draft|12=draft}}. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|12=draft|12=draft}} are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.

{{Multiple issues | One source = May 2021 | Primary sources = May 2021 }} '''Kakise Kagura''' (蛎瀬神楽) is a {{ill|Kagura|ja|神楽|12=draft|12=draft}} tradition passed down at {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja|八坂神社 (中津市蛎瀬)|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|ja|中津市|12=draft|12=draft}}, [[Ōita Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kakise Kagura is one of the {{ill|Buzen Kagura|ja|豊前神楽|12=draft|12=draft}}, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 2016. It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽|12=draft|12=draft}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽|12=draft|12=draft}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from {{ill|Mount Kubote|ja|求菩提山|12=draft|12=draft}}. According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=draft|12=draft}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]][[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神|12=draft|12=draft}}. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ|12=draft|12=draft}}, divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial {{ill|Five Elements|ja|五行|12=draft|12=draft}}. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=draft|12=draft}} slaying the {{ill|Yamata no Orochi|ja|ヤマタノオロチ|12=draft|12=draft}}. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|12=draft|12=draft}} are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.

== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|ja|産土神|12=draft|12=draft}}. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ|12=draft|12=draft}}, divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial {{ill|Five Elements|ja|五行|12=draft|12=draft}}. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=draft|12=draft}} slaying the {{ill|Yamata no Orochi|ja|ヤマタノオロチ|12=draft|12=draft}}. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Izanami|ja|イザナミ|12=draft|12=draft}} are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.
== Additional Kagura == {{section-stub}} ; San'nin Tsurugi (三人剱) ; Mikan Kagura (みかん神楽) ; Gokoku Jōju (五穀成就) ; Kenmai Kagura (献米神楽) ; Hei Kagura (幣神楽)
== External Links == * [https://kakizekagura.localinfo.jp/ Buzen Kakise Kagura] * {{Facebook|kakizekagura}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2023年12月}} '''蛎瀬神楽'''（かきせかぐら）は、[[大分県]][[中津市]][[蛎瀬]]の[[八坂神社 (中津市蛎瀬)|八坂神社]]に伝わる神楽である。
== 概要 == 2016年に「豊前神楽」の名称で国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定された神楽の一つ。中津市蛎瀬の八坂神社に伝わる豊前系の岩戸神楽で、豊前岩戸神楽蛎瀬神盛会が保護団体となっており、33番の演目を伝承している。 [[神楽]]の演目としては[[里神楽]]に分類され、内容的には伊勢流、出雲流の動作も見受けられるが駈仙（ミサキ）神楽、求菩提山の修験道の技を取り入れたものを中心としている豊前系の神楽である。 八幡古表神社（福岡県[[吉富町]]）の先祖である熊谷房重が口伝により息子房義に書かせた豊前岩戸神楽の文献によると[[求菩提山]]にまつわる「松会」の指導者であり信奉者でもあった。求菩提山に地理的にも近い[[豊前国|豊前]]一帯は、山伏の修験道の中心でもありその傘下にあった八幡古表神社宮司熊谷房重が「松会」を取り入れた神楽の手法（三十三座と火伏せの業）現在の湯立て神楽を当時の神楽方、横松芳太郎らに伝授し現在に至る。
通常の神社奉納の際には'''式神楽'''と呼ばれる壱番神楽、花神楽、手房神楽、駈仙神楽、弓正護、地割神楽、岩戸神楽は必ず舞う規則となっており式神楽の地割神楽終了より氏子が奉納する'''あげ神楽'''の奉納が可能となる。このあげ神楽と呼ばれるものは、氏子個人が奉納料を添えて神楽の舞を通じ神に厄払い、諸々の祈願等を願う意味合いを持つ。
== 三十三神楽 == {{節stub}} # '''大祓清祓（おおばらいきよみばらい）''' #: 湯庭及び神楽社中の清祓い # '''壱番神楽（いちばんかぐら）''' #: 大神の御前にて始めより終わりまで執行する神楽 # '''手房神楽（たぶさかぐら）''' #: [[笹]]二本を持ち十二干支を唱えながら舞う神楽。俗に笹神楽とも呼ぶ # '''花神楽（はなかぐら）''' #: [[皇産霊神]]より霊を花のごとく打ち散らし、村の[[産土神]]に渡す神楽 # '''二人手房（ににんたぶさ）''' #: [[幣]]と[[扇]]の舞、十二のかけ歌を唱えながら無病息災を祈願する # '''駈仙神楽（みさきかぐら）''' #: 大神ノ命を受け御先をつとめる[[猿田彦]]（サルタヒコ）、天鈿女（[[アメノウズメ]]）の舞 # '''一人剱（いちにんつるぎ）''' #: 幣、扇、[[襷]]、[[太刀]]2本を持ちて舞う # '''地割神楽（じわりかぐら）''' #: 風、火、金、水、土の五柱の神にその土地を納める神楽 # '''神宣神楽（しんせんかぐら）''' #: 幣、鈴を持って舞う。大神の命を司る # '''弓証護（ゆみしょうご）''' #: 天の[[五行]]の神に五穀豊穣を祈願する # '''乱駈仙（みだれみさき）''' #: 常に駈仙を合併し舞う。鬼（猿田彦）二人、幣方（天鈿女）二人 # '''山人舞（さんじんまい）''' #: お盆に米を入れて左右に持ち舞う。五穀豊穣を感謝する舞。俗に米神楽とも呼ぶ # '''三神舞（さんじんまい）''' #: 山、海、田の神が大神にささげる舞。餅を持って舞うことから餅神楽とも呼ぶ # '''綱駈仙（つなみさき）''' #: 大蛇を鬼神が退治する神楽 # '''二人剱（ににんつるぎ）''' #: 五つの神に祈りつつ悪気を切り払う。襷、太刀を持ち二人で舞う # '''四人剱（よにんつるぎ）''' #: 二人剱と同様の意味合いを持ち、4人で舞う # '''大蛇退治（おろちたいじ）''' #: 須左之男命[[スサノオノミコト]]が八岐大蛇[[ヤマタノオロチ]]を退治する舞 # '''神迎（かみむかえ）''' #: 伊邪那岐[[イザナギ]]、伊邪那美[[イザナミ]]の二柱の神を猿田彦が迎える舞 # '''神髄神楽（かんずいかぐら）''' #: 三人太刀を着用して十二干支を唱え湯庭の邪気を切り払い、火の神に鎮火をお願いする神楽 # '''湯の駈仙（ゆのみさき）''' #: 鬼、幣方共湯釜の邪気を払う神楽 # '''湯棒（ゆぼう）''' #: 八百万の神にささげ奉る # '''湯行事（ゆぎょうじ）''' #: 湯坪の清祓、水の初穂、真先の[[火渡り]] # '''裁量者（さいりょうしゃ）''' #: 湯庭の火番 # '''祝詞（のりと）''' #: 清祓及び[[祝詞]]を読む # '''思兼の命（[[オモイカネ]]のみこと）''' #: 岩戸の前で神々の舞を司る # '''四方鬼（しほうおに）''' #: 東方、南方、西方、北方の悪鬼 # '''鬼神退治（きしんたいじ）''' #: 刀をさばき悪鬼を切り奉納する # '''太玉命（[[フトダマ]]のみこと）''' #: 香具山の真榊を持ち神楽を奉す # '''玉祖命（[[タマノオヤ]]のみこと）''' #: 鈴と扇にて神楽を奉す # '''石凝姥命（[[イシコリドメ]]のみこと）''' #: 鏡を持ち岩戸前にて捧げ置き奉す # '''長白羽命（ナガシラハのみこと）[[長白羽神]]''' #: 弓、矢の舞を岩戸前にて奉す # '''天鈿女命（[[アメノウズメ]]のみこと）''' #: 右手に鈴、左手に扇を持ち岩戸前にて面白く舞う # '''手力男命（[[タヂカラオ]]のみこと）''' #: 大力の神で天の岩戸を開く
== 番外神楽 == {{節stub}} ;三人剱（さんにんつるぎ） ;みかん神楽（みかんかぐら） ;五穀成就（ごこくじょうじゅ） ;献米神楽（けんまいかぐら） ;幣神楽（へいかぐら）
== 外部リンク == * [https://kakizekagura.localinfo.jp/ 豊前蛎瀬神楽] * {{Facebook|kakizekagura}}
{{Pref-stub|pref=大分県}} {{Japan-culture-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:かきせかくら}} [[Category:神楽]] [[Category:八坂神社]] [[Category:中津市の文化]] [[Category:大分県の祭り]] [[Category:重要無形民俗文化財]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | One source = May 2021 | Primary sources = May 2021 }} '''Kakise Kagura''' (蛎瀬神楽) is a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] tradition passed down at {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja|八坂神社 (中津市蛎瀬)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kakise Kagura is one of the {{ill|Buzen Kagura|ja|豊前神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Kagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 2016. It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satokagura|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神楽|1=Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1348260}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from [[:en:Mount Kubote|Mount Kubote]]. According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Kobirajinja|qq=|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|qq=|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]][[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]]. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]]. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.
== Additional Kagura == {{section-stub}} ; San'nin Tsurugi (三人剱) ; Mikan Kagura (みかん神楽) ; Gokoku Jōju (五穀成就) ; Kenmai Kagura (献米神楽) ; Hei Kagura (幣神楽)
== External Links == * [https://kakizekagura.localinfo.jp/ Buzen Kakise Kagura] * {{Facebook|kakizekagura}}

The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|qq=|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.

also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]]. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]]. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | One source = May 2021 | Primary sources = May 2021 }} '''Kakise Kagura''' (蛎瀬神楽) is a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] tradition passed down at {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja|八坂神社 (中津市蛎瀬)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kakise Kagura is one of the {{ill|Buzen Kagura|ja|豊前神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 2016. It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satokagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神楽|1=Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1348260}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from [[:en:Mount Kubote|Mount Kubote]]. According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Kobirajinja|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]][[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]]. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]]. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.

Only after the Completion tier 2]][[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]]. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]]. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | One source = May 2021 | Primary sources = May 2021 }} '''Kakise Kagura''' (蛎瀬神楽) is a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] tradition passed down at {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja|八坂神社 (中津市蛎瀬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kakise Kagura is one of the {{ill|Buzen Kagura|ja|豊前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 2016. It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satokagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神楽|1=Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1348260}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from [[:en:Mount Kubote|Mount Kubote]]. According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Kobirajinja|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]][[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]]. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]]. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.

According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Kobirajinja|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]][[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]]. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]]. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.
== Additional Kagura == {{section-stub}} ; San'nin Tsurugi (三人剱) ; Mikan Kagura (みかん神楽) ; Gokoku Jōju (五穀成就) ; Kenmai Kagura (献米神楽) ; Hei Kagura (幣神楽)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | One source = May 2021 | Primary sources = May 2021 }} '''Kakise Kagura''' (蛎瀬神楽) is a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] tradition passed down at {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja|八坂神社 (中津市蛎瀬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kakise Kagura is one of the {{ill|Buzen Kagura|ja|豊前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 2016. It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satokagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神楽|1=Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1348260}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from [[:en:Mount Kubote|Mount Kubote]]. According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Kobirajinja|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]][[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.

also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]]. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | One source = May 2021 | Primary sources = May 2021 }} '''Kakise Kagura''' (蛎瀬神楽) is a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] tradition passed down at {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja|八坂神社 (中津市蛎瀬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kakise Kagura is one of the {{ill|Buzen Kagura|ja|豊前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 2016. It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satokagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神楽|1=Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1348260}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from [[:en:Mount Kubote|Mount Kubote]]. According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Kobirajinja|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]][[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]]. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]]. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.
== Additional Kagura == {{section-stub}} ; San'nin Tsurugi (三人剱) ; Mikan Kagura (みかん神楽) ; Gokoku Jōju (五穀成就) ; Kenmai Kagura (献米神楽) ; Hei Kagura (幣神楽)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | One source = May 2021 | Primary sources = May 2021 }} '''Kakise Kagura''' (蛎瀬神楽) is a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] tradition passed down at {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja|八坂神社 (中津市蛎瀬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kakise Kagura is one of the {{ill|Buzen Kagura|ja|豊前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 2016. It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satokagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神楽|1=Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1348260}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from [[:en:Mount Kubote|Mount Kubote]]. According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Kobirajinja|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]][[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]]. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]]. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.

also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]]. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.
