[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]]
<!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11625568}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Culture of Nakatsu City]] [[Category:Festivals in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Kagura]] [[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local [[:en:Ubusunagami|Ubusunagami]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:16, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11625568]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:55, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Multiple issues | One source = May 2021 | Primary sources = May 2021 }} '''Kakise Kagura''' (蛎瀬神楽) is a [[:en:Kagura|Kagura]] tradition passed down at {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja|八坂神社 (中津市蛎瀬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in [[:en:Nakatsu, Ōita|Nakatsu, Ōita]], [[:en:Ōita Prefecture|Ōita Prefecture]].
== Overview == Kakise Kagura is one of the {{ill|Buzen Kagura|ja|豊前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 2016. It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satokagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神楽|1=Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1348260}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from [[:en:Mount Kubote|Mount Kubote]]. According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Kobirajinja|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]], divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial [[:en:Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|Five Elements (Wuxing)]]. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the [[:en:Yamata no Orochi|Yamata no Orochi]]. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which [[:en:Izanagi|Izanagi]] and [[:en:Izanami|Izanami]] are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.

It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satokagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神楽|1=Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1348260}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from {{ill|Mount Kubote|en|Mount Kubote|WD=Q11550484|lt=Mount Kubote}}. According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Kobirajinja|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume}}, divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|en|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|WD=Q218652|lt=Five Elements (Wuxing)}}. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the {{ill|Yamata no Orochi|en|Yamata no Orochi|WD=Q1054437|lt=Yamata no Orochi}}. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}} and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.
== Additional Kagura == {{section-stub}} ; San'nin Tsurugi (三人剱) ; Mikan Kagura (みかん神楽) ; Gokoku Jōju (五穀成就) ; Kenmai Kagura (献米神楽) ; Hei Kagura (幣神楽)

[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11625568}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Culture of Nakatsu City]] [[Category:Festivals in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Kagura]] [[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami}} [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]]

== Overview == Kakise Kagura is one of the {{ill|Buzen Kagura|ja|豊前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 2016. It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satokagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神楽|1=Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1348260}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from {{ill|Mount Kubote|en|Mount Kubote|WD=Q11550484|lt=Mount Kubote}}. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.

Only after the Completion tier 2]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume}}, divine guides leading the way. # [[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11625568}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Culture of Nakatsu City]] [[Category:Festivals in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Kagura]] [[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]]
{{Multiple issues | One source = May 2021 | Primary sources = May 2021 }} '''Kakise Kagura''' (蛎瀬神楽) is a {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura}} tradition passed down at {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja|八坂神社 (中津市蛎瀬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|WD=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture}}.
== Overview == Kakise Kagura is one of the {{ill|Buzen Kagura|ja|豊前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 2016. It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satokagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神楽|1=Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1348260}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from {{ill|Mount Kubote|en|Mount Kubote|WD=Q11550484|lt=Mount Kubote}}. According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Kobirajinja|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume}}, divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|en|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|WD=Q218652|lt=Five Elements (Wuxing)}}. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the {{ill|Yamata no Orochi|en|Yamata no Orochi|WD=Q1054437|lt=Yamata no Orochi}}. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi}} and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami}} are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.
== Additional Kagura == {{section-stub}} ; San'nin Tsurugi (三人剱) ; Mikan Kagura (みかん神楽) ; Gokoku Jōju (五穀成就) ; Kenmai Kagura (献米神楽) ; Hei Kagura (幣神楽)
== External Links == * [https://kakizekagura.localinfo.jp/ Buzen Kakise Kagura] * {{Facebook|kakizekagura}}

Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]]

'''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.
== Additional Kagura == {{section-stub}} ; San'nin Tsurugi (三人剱) ; Mikan Kagura (みかん神楽) ; Gokoku Jōju (五穀成就) ; Kenmai Kagura (献米神楽) ; Hei Kagura (幣神楽)
== External Links == * [https://kakizekagura.localinfo.jp/ Buzen Kakise Kagura] * {{Facebook|kakizekagura}}
{{Pref-stub|pref=Ōita Prefecture}} {{Japan-culture-stub}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Buddhism-stub}}
{{translated page|ja|蛎瀬神楽|version=98533873|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11625568}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Culture of Nakatsu City]] [[Category:Festivals in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Kagura]] [[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]]
{{Multiple issues | One source = May 2021 | Primary sources = May 2021 }} '''Kakise Kagura''' (蛎瀬神楽) is a {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|WD=Q1348260|lt=Kagura|1=Kagura}} tradition passed down at {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja|八坂神社 (中津市蛎瀬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|WD=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu, Ōita|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|WD=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}.
== Overview == Kakise Kagura is one of the {{ill|Buzen Kagura|ja|豊前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 2016. It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satokagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神楽|1=Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1348260}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from {{ill|Mount Kubote|en|Mount Kubote|WD=Q11550484|lt=Mount Kubote|1=Mount Kubote}}. According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Kobirajinja|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|1=Ame-no-Uzume}}, divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|en|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|WD=Q218652|lt=Five Elements (Wuxing)|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}}. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the {{ill|Yamata no Orochi|en|Yamata no Orochi|WD=Q1054437|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=Yamata no Orochi}}. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.

According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Kobirajinja|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131274484}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11563776}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|WD=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|1=Ame-no-Uzume}}, divine guides leading the way. # '''Ichinin Tsurugi (一人剱)''' #: A solo dance using a sacred wand, fan, sash, and two swords. # '''Jiwari Kagura (地割神楽)''' #: A dance offering land to the five elemental gods (wind, fire, metal, water, earth). # '''Shinsen Kagura (神宣神楽)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and bells, proclaiming the divine will. # '''Yumi Shōgo (弓証護)''' #: A dance praying for bountiful harvests, dedicated to the celestial {{ill|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|en|Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)|WD=Q218652|lt=Five Elements (Wuxing)|1=Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)}}. # '''Midare Misaki (乱駈仙)''' #: A variation of Misaki Kagura performed by two oni (Sarutahiko) and two sacred dancers (Ame-no-Uzume). # '''Sanjinmai (山人舞)''' #: A dance of gratitude for abundant harvests, also called Kome Kagura (Rice Kagura), performed with a tray of rice. # '''Sanjinmai (三神舞)''' #: A dance offering to the gods of mountains, seas, and fields, performed with rice cakes; also called Mochi Kagura. # '''Tsunamisaki (綱駈仙)''' #: A kagura depicting an oni subduing a great serpent. # '''Ninin Tsurugi (二人剱)''' #: A dance where two performers use swords to ward off evil, praying to five deities. # '''Yonin Tsurugi (四人剱)''' #: A four-person variation of Ninin Tsurugi. # '''Orochi Taiji (大蛇退治)''' #: A dance depicting {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|スサノオノミコト|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} slaying the {{ill|Yamata no Orochi|en|Yamata no Orochi|WD=Q1054437|lt=Yamata no Orochi|1=Yamata no Orochi}}. # '''Kami Mukae (神迎)''' #: A dance in which {{ill|Izanagi|en|Izanagi|WD=Q813858|lt=Izanagi|1=Izanagi}} and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|WD=Q682306|lt=Izanami|1=Izanami}} are welcomed by Sarutahiko. # '''Kanzui Kagura (神髄神楽)''' #: A dance purifying the ritual grounds, invoking fire deities for protection. # '''Yunomisaki (湯の駈仙)''' #: A purification ritual for the sacred cauldron. # '''Yubō (湯棒)''' #: A sacred offering to the eight million gods. # '''Yugyōji (湯行事)''' #: Ritual purification of the hot springs, involving first fruits and fire-walking. # '''Sairyōsha (裁量者)''' #: The firekeeper overseeing the ritual grounds. # '''Norito (祝詞)''' #: Chanting of purification prayers. # '''Omoikane no Mikoto (思兼の命)''' #: A dance presided over by the god of wisdom at the celestial cave. # '''Shihō Oni (四方鬼)''' #: A dance featuring oni representing the four cardinal directions. # '''Kishin Taiji (鬼神退治)''' #: A sword dance symbolizing the vanquishing of evil spirits. # '''Futodama no Mikoto (太玉命)''' #: A dance offering the sacred sakaki tree from Mount Kagu. # '''Tamanooya no Mikoto (玉祖命)''' #: A ritual dance using bells and a fan. # '''Ishikoridome no Mikoto (石凝姥命)''' #: A dance presenting a mirror before the celestial cave. # '''Nagashiraha no Mikoto (長白羽命)''' #: A bow and arrow dance performed at the celestial cave. # '''Ame-no-Uzume no Mikoto (天鈿女命)''' #: A dance of joyful performance using bells and a fan. # '''Tajikarao no Mikoto (手力男命)''' #: A powerful dance symbolizing the opening of the celestial cave.

[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11625568}} [[Category:Culture of Nakatsu City]] [[Category:Festivals in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Kagura]] [[Category:of Jiwari Kagura can '''Age Kagura''' (あげ神楽) be performed. Age Kagura consists of performances dedicated by parishioners with offerings to the kami, serving as a form of purification and prayer.
== The Thirty-Three Kagura == {{section-stub}} # '''Ōbarai Kiyomibari (大祓清祓)''' #: Purification of the ritual grounds and kagura performers. # '''Ichiban Kagura (壱番神楽)''' #: The first kagura performed before the gods, from beginning to end. # '''Tebusa Kagura (手房神楽)''' #: A kagura performed with two stalks of bamboo, chanting the twelve zodiac signs; also called Sasa Kagura. # '''Hana Kagura (花神楽)''' #: A ritual where spiritual energy is scattered like flowers and delivered to the local {{ill|Ubusunagami|en|Ubusunagami|WD=Q1392097|lt=Ubusunagami|1=Ubusunagami}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Yasaka-jinja]] [[Category:Pages with 50+ untranslated japanese characters]] [[Category:Pages with 100+ untranslated japanese characters]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:蛎瀬神楽]]
{{Multiple issues | One source = May 2021 | Primary sources = May 2021 }} '''Kakise Kagura''' (蛎瀬神楽) is a {{ill|Kagura|en|Kagura|qid=Q1348260|lt=Kagura|1=Kagura}} tradition passed down at {{ill|Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja|八坂神社 (中津市蛎瀬)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasaka Shrine (Nakatsu, Kakise)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in {{ill|Nakatsu, Ōita|en|Nakatsu, Ōita|qid=Q861221|lt=Nakatsu, Ōita|1=Nakatsu, Ōita}}, {{ill|Ōita Prefecture|en|Ōita Prefecture|qid=Q133924|lt=Ōita Prefecture|1=Ōita Prefecture}}.
== Overview == Kakise Kagura is one of the {{ill|Buzen Kagura|ja|豊前神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Buzen Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, designated as a [[National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property]] in 2016. It is a Buzen-style {{ill|Iwatokagura|ja|岩戸神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatokagura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} performed at Yasaka Shrine in Kakise, Nakatsu City, and is preserved by the Buzen Iwatokagura Kakise Shinseikai. The tradition includes 33 performances.
As a type of {{ill|Satokagura|ja|里神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Satokagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神楽|1=Kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1348260}}, its content incorporates movements from the Ise and Izumo schools but primarily follows the Misaki Kagura and Shugendō techniques from {{ill|Mount Kubote|en|Mount Kubote|qid=Q11550484|lt=Mount Kubote|1=Mount Kubote}}. According to the historical records written by Kumagai Fusayoshi, son of Kumagai Fusaharu of {{ill|Hachiman Kobirajinja|ja|八幡古表神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Kobirajinja|1=Hachiman Kohyou Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131274484}} (Yoshitomi, Fukuoka Prefecture), these techniques were integrated into the local kagura. Kumagai, a practitioner of the Matsue ritual, was also a leader and devotee of Mount Kubote's Shugendō. The shrine priest of Hachiman Kobirajinja passed down the kagura techniques, including the "Thirty-Three Seats" and the fire-prevention rituals, which evolved into the current {{ill|Yutate Kagura|ja|湯立神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yutate Kagura|1=yudate kagura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11563776}}.
During shrine offerings, certain performances known as '''Shiki Kagura''' (式神楽) must always be performed, including Ichiban Kagura, Hana Kagura, Tebusa Kagura, Misaki Kagura, Yumi Shōgo, Jiwari Kagura, and Iwato Kagura. Only after the Completion tier 2]]. # '''Ninin Tebusa (二人手房)''' #: A dance using a sacred wand and fan, chanting twelve verses for health and safety. # '''Misaki Kagura (駈仙神楽)''' #: A dance by {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} and {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|en|Ame-no-Uzume|qid=Q462803|lt=Ame-no-Uzume|1=Ame-no-Uzume}}, divine guides leading the way. #
